Name the service whatever you like, you will reference this within your mta.yaml file, and in your logic file as we saw earlier.

For the parameters, provide one field for host, and the value of the host will be the connection string we saved way back in the beginning.

 

 

We have a couple of different ways to build and deploy this application, since we’re in SAP Build Code we have MBT (MTA Build Tools) which is used to compile this project, available out of the box.

We can compile the project either via the command line, or by right clicking the mta.yaml file, and selecting the build command.

 

 

To compile the project via the command line, ensure you’re in the directory with your mta.yaml file, and type mbt build.

 

 

Whichever way you choose to build your project, the outcome is the same. It compiles your project into an mtar file, mtar meaning MTA Archive. This is what we will actually deploy to Cloud Foundry. This file resides in a newly created folder for your mtar files.

 

 

Same as with building our project, we can deploy our project either by right clicking our newly created mtar file, or by using the Cloud Foundry command line interface. For either option, ensure you are first logged into the Cloud Foundry by performing a cf login command in the terminal.

 

 

For the first option, simply right click the file, and select the deploy option.

 

 

For using the command line, navigate into your mta_archives folder, and type cf deploy <mtar file name>.mtar. This will trigger the same process as if you right clicked the file.

 

 

 

 

Once we see the project has successfully deployed, let’s navigate back to our Cloud Foundry Space, and notice in the Applications section, we have our service layer deployed here.

 

 

Don’t get worried about your UI layer not being present. Recall we deployed using a Managed Approuter, which means that it doesn’t live here, but in the HTML5 Apps Repo.

 

 

NOTE : In order to view apps deployed with a Managed Approuter in the Subaccount, you need to have a subscription to SAP Build Work Zone, either Standard or Advanced Edition.

Click on the link for your application, and see your UI we had tested earlier locally, now publicly available on your BTP landscape.

entry.title: The Role of SAP Master Data Governance within the SAP Business Suite Strategy
entry.content:
In today’s data-driven business landscape, organizations struggle with data they do not trust, affecting their ability to innovate and harness the full potential of data and AI. A recent global survey highlighted that 55% of business and technology leaders cite poor data quality as their biggest challenge, with nearly half also pointing to the difficulties in harmonizing data across ecosystems as a major barrier to innovation. Data-oriented organizations, however, are found to be four times more likely to succeed compared to those in early data management maturity stages.

The Right Data Foundation

Business users require real-time, trusted, and contextually rich data for analytics and planning needs. SAP believes this necessitates seamless integration between all data platforms and business applications, underpinned by a consistent domain data model. SAP’s not only ensures data product consistency but also simplifies master data integration, providing a common language across the entire application landscape to ensure seamless, end-to-end business process execution.  In addition, with the rapid growth in the realm and possibilities of AI, this also requires organizations to first have the right data foundation.  And, as we know, the most important data in that foundation is your business-critical master data.

SAP Business Suite

SAP recently unveiled its new vision to integrate applications, data, and AI under the umbrella of the SAP Business Suite. This suite is not just a collection of solutions, but a comprehensive transformation engine designed to foster continuous innovation and improvement.

Here is how the SAP Business Suite works and how SAP Master Data Governance plays a role:

Unparalleled Applications (including integration and governance):

SAP’s business applications and Business Technology Platform (BTP) are tightly integrated, ensuring seamless, end-to-end business process execution and not just a set of isolated applications. Accordingly, SAP MDG is designed to offer deep integration within SAP applications rather than functioning merely as an adjacent tool. Of course, SAP MDG can be a master data management solution for any heterogeneous application landscape, but in the context of SAP applications, we can offer much more integration. We have shown this with scenarios embedded into SAP S/4HANA, or with federated SAP MDG deployments across an application landscape.  SAP Master Data Governance also helps to ensure that every action taken within these applications – as well as other points of entry - generates trusted, business-critical master data at its source.  SAP Master Data Governance ensures replication of high-quality master data across the suite to maintain data accuracy, consistency, and integrity.

Unmatched Data (along with data centralization and enrichment):

We then centralize, harmonize, and enrich master, transactional, 3rd party and other types & sources of data in SAP Business Data Cloud. It doesn’t matter where it’s generated, SAP or non-SAP systems, we unify it into a single source of truth. Contextualized, reliable, and accessible, this data becomes the fuel for advanced 360-degree or AI-driven insights.  SAP Master Data Governance supports this 360-degree or unified version of the truth, though master data duplication checks within the master data creation and consolidation processes. By creating a single, authoritative record (or “best record”), SAP MDG eliminates duplicate data entries and inconsistencies. 

Unrivaled AI (with a virtuous cycle of innovation):

While much of SAP MDG’s focus for AI is on automation and efficiency (e.g. GenAI for assisted master data changes); SAP Business AI goes beyond basic automation. SAP Business AI and Joule allow for Business-AI processes that are embedded directly into core operations, optimizing and automating repetitive processes in real-time, boosting productivity and efficiency. Through Joule, our AI copilot, employees can orchestrate intelligent agents to collaborate across functions. This breaks down silos, driving real-time, cross-enterprise optimization. Where others may deploy AI in pockets, SAP weaves it throughout the organization, unlocking value and resilience at scale. And as AI refines processes, they generate richer data that fuels even smarter decisions - creating that virtuous cycle of innovation that no one else can match.

SAP Business Data Cloud

The SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) unifies and governs data from various sources, bridging data silos and providing a trusted and harmonized data foundation, empowering IT and business leaders with full business context to drive more impactful decisions.  SAP BDC is SAP’s unified data platform.  SAP MDG plugs into this platform by delivering high quality, master data across the SAP Business Suite - maintaining business-critical master data consistency, accuracy, and integrity. Business Data Cloud helps businesses unleash transformative new insights with prebuilt analytical applications and curated SAP data products.  And it provides the trusted data foundation to deliver the most reliable AI in our industry.

Data products are the building blocks for intelligent applications.  They are purpose built by line of business experts to help customers get the most out of their data.  And they solve the difficult task of bringing all their SAP data together in a single data platform while maintaining the meaning of that data and simplifying data management. SAP’s pre-built, fully managed data products will provide a 360-degree business view of a customer’s most critical business processes.

These curated data products maintain the original business context and semantics and align to that highly optimized and unified one domain model, the quality of those core master data attributes is managed with SAP MDG. Data products are included in the Business Data Cloud catalog making them easy to discover, use, and extend.  These data products are also consumed by BDC Insight Apps, which are pre-built analytical applications tailored to line of business analytics - such as People, Customer, or Supply Chain analytics – supporting 360-degree insights. In the future, SAP Master Data Governance can further contribute to these data products with additional master data metadata, like data quality scores, rule infractions, or whether a record is indeed the best record.

Conclusion

SAP’s Business Suite strategy, with SAP Master Data Governance supporting business-critical master data quality, represents a holistic approach to data management and innovation. By seamlessly integrating applications, data, and AI, SAP empowers businesses to trust their data, make smarter decisions, and drive continuous improvement. This approach underscores SAP’s commitment to creating a virtuous cycle of innovation, setting the standard for what organizations can achieve with a robust data foundation.

Discover how to get more complete, accurate, and compliant enterprise data with a modern master data management (MDM) system like SAP Master Data Governance. Explore what makes up an MDM system and what its role is in data fabric and AI initiatives.

  • Read the guide: https://www.sap.com/documents/2024/08/8aff13da-d17e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html
  • Listen to the AI-generated podcast: https://letstalkdatapodcast.podbean.com/e/the-evolving-role-of-mdm-in-modern-data-management/

entry.title: SAP Business Data Fabric: Unifying Your Enterprise Data with BDC (Business Data Cloud)
entry.content:
SAP Business Data Fabric offers a modern approach to enterprise data management that breaks down silos and creates a unified data ecosystem. This framework helps businesses connect their scattered data resources into a cohesive, accessible whole.

At its core, SAP Business Data Cloud connects data from everywhere—cloud systems, on-premise databases, and hybrid environments—creating a single, comprehensive view of your organization's information. This unified approach eliminates the traditional barriers between data sources.

 

SAP Business Data Fabric and SAP Business Data Cloud

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) serves as the cloud-native implementation layer of the Business Data Fabric strategy. While the Business Data Fabric provides the architectural framework 🏗️ and methodologies, BDC delivers the technology stack 🛠️ that makes this vision a reality.

 

How They Work Together 🤝

  • Business Data Fabric: Defines the conceptual framework and principles.

  • Business Data Cloud: Implements these concepts with scalable cloud solutions.

  • Together: They form a complete solution for modern enterprise data management.

This integration enables organizations to leverage cloud scalability while maintaining the comprehensive data governance and integration capabilities of the data fabric approach.

 

The framework excels in several key areas: 

  • Seamless Integration - Connect data across your entire organization regardless of where it lives, with BDC providing cloud-optimized connection points.
  • Strong Governance - Maintain high data quality while ensuring regulatory compliance and security across both on-premise and cloud environments.
  • Real-Time Capabilities - Access the latest information when you need it through BDC's cloud-based processing power.
  • AI-Powered Insights - Leverage artificial intelligence within the BDC environment to uncover patterns and opportunities in your data.
  • Enterprise Scalability - Grow your data infrastructure alongside your business, utilizing BDC's elastic cloud resources.
  • Democratized Access - Put data in the hands of everyone who needs it through BDC's user-friendly cloud interfaces.

 

By combining SAP Business Data Fabric's strategic framework with SAP Business Data Cloud's advanced technology, organizations can:

  • Make smarter decisions faster ⚡

  • Streamline operations 🛠️

  • Innovate confidently in today’s hybrid IT landscape 🌍

This unified approach ensures that your enterprise is ready to thrive in the era of data-driven transformation!

Find out more on Data Fabric: What is a data fabric? | SAP

entry.title: Introducing the Gantt Chart Custom Widget for SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Intoduction

While SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) provides powerful tools for visualizing and analyzing data, one notable visualization currently missing from its standard offering is the Gantt Chart. Gantt charts are widely used for visualizing timelines, dependencies, and resource allocation, making them indispensable for effective project management and scheduling.

To bridge this gap, I've developed a custom Gantt Chart widget specifically designed for SAC users. This widget seamlessly integrates into your SAC environment.

SAP users across various business functions can leverage this custom widget to visualize numerous use cases, including:

  • Project Timelines
  • Maintenance Scheduling
  • Production Planning
  • Employee Resource Allocation
  • Release Management

In this blog, you'll learn step-by-step how to integrate and configure this custom Gantt Chart widget in your SAC environment to effectively visualize and manage your scheduling data.

 

Installing the Gantt Chart Custom Widget

To integrate the Gantt Chart custom widget into your SAP Analytics Cloud environment, please follow these steps:

  1. Download Required Files: Download UI5GanttChart.json and UI5GanttChart.zip files from the GitHub repository.

  2. Upload JSON Definition File:

    • Log in to your SAP Analytics Cloud.
    • Navigate to Stories and select the Custom Widgets tab.
    • Click Create and upload the UI5GanttChart.json file you downloaded earlier.

  3. Upload Resource Files:

    • After uploading the JSON file, click the Select File button that appears.
    • Select and upload the UI5GanttChart.zip archive file containing the widget's resource files.

For additional information, refer to the official SAP Analytics Cloud documentation.

 

Technical Implementation Overview

The Gantt Chart custom widget is built using the standard UI5 GanttChartWithTable control. Some properties of the underlying UI5 control are exposed through the widget's Builder and Styling panels, allowing you to customize and adapt the visualization according to your needs. However, the UI5 GanttChartWithTable control offers numerous additional features, many of which are not yet implemented in this custom widget. This provides significant opportunities for future improvements and feature enhancements. A detailed discussion of the technical implementation will be covered in a separate blog post.

 

Data Binding for the Custom Widget

The Gantt Chart custom widget does not utilize standard SAC data binding capabilities like those available for table or chart widgets. Instead, it provides an API method named setDataSet that accepts one argument of type Array of ResultSet. You can easily obtain this data array by retrieving it from a standard SAC table widget. Specifically, call the getDataSource().getResultSet() method on the table widget, then pass the result directly to the custom widget using the following code:

var tasksResultSet = Table_Tasks.getDataSource().getResultSet();
GanttChart_1.setDataSet(tasksResultSet);

This approach requires you to use a standard table to retrieve the necessary data, even if this table is hidden from view. It's convenient to implement the provided code snippet within the onResultChanged event handler of the table, ensuring that each time the table data changes (e.g., applying a new filter), the Gantt Chart widget updates automatically.

Because of this approach, data will not be visualized in the Gantt Chart in design mode (designtime).

The custom widget also needs to understand how to extract information from the ResultSet. Thus, required fields need to be mapped to corresponding elements in the ResultSet through the "Dataset Parsing Settings" section in the widget's Builder Panel.

To inspect your ResultSet structure, you can use the following script to log it to the console:

console.log(tasksResultSet);

Here is an example of what your ResultSet might look like:

[
    {
        "@MeasureDimension": {
            "id": "DUMMY",
            "description": "DUMMY",
            "rawValue": "1",
            "formattedValue": "1"
        },
        "ID": {
            "id": "1",
            "description": "1",
            "properties": {}
        },
        "Name": {
            "id": "Phase 1",
            "description": "Phase 1",
            "properties": {}
        },
        "Description": {
            "id": "Initial phase of the project",
            "description": "Initial phase of the project",
            "properties": {}
        },
        "Start_Datetime": {
            "id": "2025-01-01 08:00:00.0000000",
            "description": "Jan 1, 2025 8:00:00",
            "properties": {}
        },
        "End_Datetime": {
            "id": "2025-03-31 18:00:00.0000000",
            "description": "Mar 31, 2025 18:00:00",
            "properties": {}
        },
        "Parent_ID": {
            "id": "",
            "description": "",
            "properties": {}
        },
        "Color": {
            "id": "#3357FF",
            "description": "#3357FF",
            "properties": {}
        }
    },
    {
        "@MeasureDimension": {
            "id": "DUMMY",
            "description": "DUMMY",
            "rawValue": "1",
            "formattedValue": "1"
        },
        "ID": {
            "id": "5",
            "description": "5",
            "properties": {}
        },
        "Name": {
            "id": "Task 1.1",
            "description": "Task 1.1",
            "properties": {}
        },
        "Description": {
            "id": "Research and planning",
            "description": "Research and planning",
            "properties": {}
        },
        "Start_Datetime": {
            "id": "2025-01-05 09:00:00.0000000",
            "description": "Jan 5, 2025 9:00:00",
            "properties": {}
        },
        "End_Datetime": {
            "id": "2025-01-25 17:00:00.0000000",
            "description": "Jan 25, 2025 17:00:00",
            "properties": {}
        },
        "Parent_ID": {
            "id": "1",
            "description": "1",
            "properties": {}
        },
        "Color": {
            "id": "#3357FF",
            "description": "#3357FF",
            "properties": {}
        }
    },
...
]

And the corresponding settings in the Builder panel:

Values in the ResultSet can be addressed in the following ways:

  • Name.id – the id of the corresponding dimension will be used
  • Name.description – the description of the corresponding dimension will be used
  • Name – equivalent to Name.id

Below is an overview of each field required for configuring the Gantt Chart custom widget datset parsing:

  • Event ID: Unique identifier for each bar on the Gantt Chart.
  • Event Name: Text displayed directly on the bar of the Gantt Chart.
  • Event Description (optional): Text displayed as a tooltip when hovering over a bar.
  • Event Start Time, Event End Time: Define the start and end times corresponding to the Gantt Chart bar.
  • Parent ID (optional): Enables hierarchical organization of bars into a two-level structure. This field should reference the Event ID of the parent bar.
  • Color of Event Bar (optional): Hexadecimal color code used to define the color of the corresponding bar on the Gantt Chart.

 

Additional Settings

The Gantt Chart custom widget provides additional configuration options to fine-tune its appearance and behavior. These settings are available in the widget's Builder panel. Styling-related settings will be covered in a separate section.

 

Time Zone Settings

  • Dataset Timezone Offset from UTC (UTC ± X): If the value is not zero, the Start Time and End Time will be converted from the specified offset to UTC. This setting can also be used to visually shift the Gantt bars by a defined number of hours.

 

Horizon Settings

Total Horizon defines the complete time frame that is visible in the Gantt Chart. It can either be fixed or dynamic:

  • If Dynamic Total Horizon is disabled, the horizon boundaries are defined by the values set in Start Time and End Time properties.
  • If Dynamic Total Horizon is enabled, the horizon is calculated based on the current date and time, using the specified number of minutes to look back and forward (Look Back and Look Forward properties).

 

Visual Horizon defines the portion of the Total Horizon that is visible to the initially visible to the user.

  • If Dynamic Visual Horizon is enabled, its boundaries are calculated automatically to ensure all bars from the dataset are included and visible in the chart area by default.
  • Otherwise, the visual horizon is equal to the total horizon.

 

Display Mode

  • Compact Representation: When enabled, all bars with the same name will be displayed on a single row, while still maintaining unique IDs. This is especially useful for visualizing recurring events or grouped tasks. If disabled, each bar is shown on its own row even if multiple bars share the same name.

Compact Representation

 

Table Settings

  • Table Columns: This property defines the columns that appear in the table section (left pane) of the Gantt Chart. It expects a JSON array of objects, each specifying a column with two required properties:
    • field: Refers to a dimension in the dataset in the same way as parsing settings (either id or description).
    • label: The display name shown in the table header.

Example:

[
  {
    "field": "Name",
    "label": "Tasks"
  },
  {
    "field": "Description",
    "label": "Description"
  },
  {
    "field": "Start_Datetime.description",
    "label": "Start"
  },
  {
    "field": "End_Datetime.description",
    "label": "End"
  }
]

 

Gantt Chart Styling

The Gantt Chart custom widget offers several visual customization options located in the widget's Styling panel. These settings allow to control the appearance of the chart bars and labels for improved readability and alignment with visual design standards.

 

Apply Same Color for All Bars

  • This setting enables users to define a uniform color for all bars in the chart.
  • It accepts a hex color code (e.g., #3498DB).
  • When applied, the color defined here overrides the color values provided in the dataset.

 

Automatic Text Color

  • A checkbox that, when enabled, automatically selects either white or black text based on the bar color to ensure readability.
  • This option is useful when bar colors vary and text contrast must be preserved.

 

Fixed Text Color

  • This field is only active when Automatic Text Color is disabled.
  • Users can manually define a hex color code to be used for text labels across all bars.

Bars Opacity

  • Defines the transparency level of the bars. Default value is 0.8.
  • Accepts a numeric value from 0 (completely transparent) to 1 (fully opaque).

 

Conclusion

The Gantt Chart custom widget for SAP Analytics Cloud fills a critical visualization gap by enabling intuitive and flexible timeline representation directly within SAC stories and applications. With its support for hierarchical structures, dynamic time horizons, and extensive customization options, the widget seamlessly integrates into diverse planning and reporting scenarios.

By following the setup and configuration steps outlined in this post, you can easily incorporate the widget into your dashboards.

Further enhancements and deep-dives into the technical implementation will be covered in upcoming posts. Feedback and contributions are welcome — feel free to comment and help shape the future of this custom visualization.

entry.title: 3 Reasons to Register for the Modernizing SAP BW with SAP Business Data Cloud Webinar
entry.content:
SAP Business Warehouse has been a cornerstone data warehousing application for organization across industries for over 25 years. However, as market requirements and business needs continue to evolve, there has been a shift from traditional IT-centric systems to comprehensive multi-cloud architectures.

If you’re looking to modernize to the cloud while retaining the value of your SAP BW data for re-use, mark your calendar for SAP’s upcoming webinar: Modernizing SAP Business Warehouse with SAP Business Data Cloud. We are excited to have Accenture as a partner in this session, where we’ll explore how you can minimize migration risks and preserve your valuable SAP BW or SAP BW/4HANA objects in SAP Business Data Cloud.

Why should you register? Here are the top three reasons:

1. Explore a streamlined modernization approach to the cloud

We understand that your SAP BW system is integral to your data ecosystem, and significant resources have been invested in it over the years. In this webinar, you will discover how you can preserve these investments with a streamlined modernization approach to the cloud. Learn how SAP Business Data Cloud allows you to modernization your SAP BW data into a private cloud environment fully managed by SAP, enabling you to deliver your SAP BW data as data products. This approach provides a flexible framework to adopt SAP Business Data Cloud at your own pace – without the need to rebuild models or objects using a third-party solution.

2. Maximize the value of your SAP BW data with SAP Business Data Cloud

See how you can unlock more value from your SAP BW data with the latest data and AI capabilities in SAP Business Data Cloud. Learn how to unleash transformative insights with SAP managed data products, intelligent apps, and support for data engineering and advanced AI/ML workloads in SAP Databricks.

3. See it in action

Often, the best way to understand how a system works is to see it live. We’ll walk you through a product demo showcasing the end-to-end processing of provisioning your SAP BW objects with the data product generator in SAP Business Data Cloud. See how easy it can be to leverage your SAP BW data in the cloud while maintaining its business context and value.

Secure your spot now!

Don’t miss out on this opportunity to explore how you can get started with modernizing your SAP BW system with SAP Business Data Cloud to unleash the power of AI with trusted business data. Register today to secure your spot and take the first step towards transforming your SAP BW data for the future!

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud and what it means for SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
I have been in many customer discussions over the past weeks since SAP Business Data Cloud was launched on February 13, 2025 as part of the business unleashed event. People always ask similar questions, also during the technology days of the German SAP user group DSAG last week which led me to create this blog to reach a broader audience to clarify regular questions we get and help you find answers to avoid confusion.

 

Where do I find more information about the product and the announcement?

With the announcement there are new pages on our corporate sap.com website as well as a new page on the SAP community with lots of details about the new solution:

📺 Watch the replay of the launch event

🔗 Check out the product page overview on sap.com

🔗 Join the SAP Community for exchange with your peers

 

What is SAP Business Data Cloud and why does it matter?

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) is a fully managed Software as a Service (SaaS) solution that unifies and governs all SAP data while seamlessly connecting with third-party data. It provides line-of-business leaders with the context needed to make impactful decisions. The solution combines the functionalities of SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Business Warehouse, along with managed SAP Databricks and context aware Insight Apps. It includes curated business-ready data products from SAP line of business applications.

SAP Business Data Cloud is at the core of SAP's Business Suite strategy, which aims to provide a comprehensive view of business operations by integrating applications, data, and AI. It brings together the world’s most comprehensive and mission-critical data with the leading data platform to deliver transformational insights across all lines of business.

This integration helps businesses manage complexity and interconnected processes more effectively, enabling smarter decision-making and optimized business outcomes.

🔗 Discover more details on the eBook online.

📺 For a more detailed view on BDC watch my overview session on YouTube

 

SAP Business Data Cloud specific questions

Which components are part of SAP BDC?

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) is a fully managed SaaS solution that comprises several components that are integral to its functionality. These components include:

  1. SAP Datasphere: This component is part of the BDC offering, providing capabilities for data modeling and business semantics, data integration and data management, as well as for the definition of custom data products.
  2. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC): SAC is included in BDC to offer advanced analytics, visualization and planning tools.
  3. SAP BW 7.5 / SAP BW/4HANA private cloud: This component is part of the BDC to support data warehousing needs and the path forward for existing on-premises SAP Business Warehouse customers, while being able to leverage the data product generator to create custom data products based on BW data and semantics.
  4. SAP Databricks: This SAP managed component is included to provide data science, data engineering and machine learning / artificial intelligence (ML/AI) capabilities. Also here you will be able to create your custom data products shared via zero copy delta share.
  5. Insight apps and data products: These are prebuilt apps that consume SAP managed and curated data products to provide analytical, process, or domain insights, and access via zero copy delta share.
  6. Data catalog: The data catalog is a central component that supports federated metadata management providing a unified view of metadata from various data sources. This central catalog is complemented by metadata APIs, which facilitate integration with other catalog systems. The catalog is a crucial component for managing and discovering data products within the BDC environment, ensuring that users can efficiently access and utilize the data they need for their business processes.
  7. Business AI with Joule: AI infusion with Joule and its agents leverage the data foundation provided by SAP BDC and its embedded SAP Knowledge Graph technology.
  8. SAP Business Data Cloud cockpit: It is designed to facilitate administrative tasks such as the installation of Data Packages and Insight Apps. This cockpit serves as a centralized platform for managing various components and functionalities within the Business Data Cloud, streamlining the process for users to perform necessary administrative operations efficiently.

These components work together to unify and govern all SAP data while seamlessly connecting with third-party data, providing a comprehensive solution for data and analytics.

 

What is new with data products?

SAP data products are a central feature in SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), that provide curated and enriched data sets for various analytical and business needs. Data products are not a new concept to SAP and they are a core part of the data marketplace of SAP Datasphere for custom-built data products.

Here are some of the new aspects and functionalities related to data products in BDC:

  1. Curated and Enriched Data: BDC offers SAP managed curated and enriched data products that are designed to provide advanced analytics and cross-analytics capabilities. These data products are intended to help users make smart decisions and optimize business outcomes.
  2. Plug-and-Play on a Harmonized Data Model: SAP data products are delivered out-of-the-box using an always-on experience and following a harmonized data model to provide scalable access without data duplication. This not only includes metadata, but also includes the data of connected customer systems like from SAP S/4HANA PCE.
  3. Integration with Insight Apps: SAP data products in BDC are consumed by Insight Apps, which provide analytical, process, or domain insights. Currently these apps cover analytic models in Datasphere and pre-defined SAC stories, with the intention to expand the app coverage beyond analytical use cases and also leverage pro-code applications built on SAP BTP.
  4. Data Sharing and Consumption: BDC facilitates the sharing of data products between applications, allowing for seamless data integration and consumption leveraging zero copy delta share. This is part of the broader goal to enable easy data sharing and consumption across different platforms and applications avoiding the need for countless data copies, while using the same data for multiple use cases and tools.

 

 

What are these insight apps?

Insight Apps in SAP Business Data Cloud are low code apps designed to deliver tailored insights and actionable recommendations out-of-the-box. They are a collection of pre-built apps that leverage SAP's curated unified semantic models. These apps are integrated with essential business applications to automate critical daily decisions in various areas such as working capital management, sales and distribution, and people analytics.

They contain an SAP managed collection of analytical assets and functionalities to provide value to customers through analytical, process, or domain insights. They can contain data products, semantic models, stories, planning models, AI-powered analytics, and more.

 

 

SAP Datasphere related Questions

What does that mean for existing SAP Datasphere customers?

For existing SAP Datasphere customers this means you are on the right track with your investment in SAP Datasphere. There will be no immediate changes, and existing customers can continue using their current services without interruption.

Over time, existing customers will have the option to transition to the fully managed solution offered by SAP Business Data Cloud. This transition is designed to be simple and ideally seamless, ensuring that customers do not experience any disruption in their services.

The initial release of SAP Business Data Cloud for controlled availability in Q1 2025 is mainly addressing greenfield customers. Later in 2025 existing SAP Datasphere customers can also start adopting SAP Business Data Cloud and bring their existing landscape into the fully managed SaaS experience.

 

Can existing SAP Datasphere systems be used without SAP BDC?

Yes, existing SAP Datasphere systems can continue to be used without transitioning to SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC). Customers currently using SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud services can continue their operations without interruption. Over time, they will have the option to transition to the fully managed solution offered by SAP Business Data Cloud, but it is not mandatory. This ensures that existing customers can maintain their current setups and workflows without being forced to migrate to BDC.

 

How can an existing SAP Datasphere system be integrated into SAP BDC?

Existing SAP Datasphere systems can be onboarded into SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) through a service that will be available at a later point in time. This onboarding will allow existing Datasphere customers to transition to BDC, leveraging the new functionalities and services offered by BDC while maintaining their current data and workflows. The onboarding process is designed to be simple and ideally seamless, ensuring that customers do not experience any disruption in their services.

 

Is the SAP BW Bridge of SAP Datasphere still supported in SAP BDC?

Yes, the SAP BW Bridge is still supported in the context of SAP Business Data Cloud. However, the strategic solution for SAP BW customers is to move to SAP BW Cloud, private edition to accelerate adoption of SAP Business Data Cloud. If customers envision a decommissioning of their SAP BW systems and the SAP BW investment can be covered within the scope of SAP BW Bridge, customers can replace the SAP BW 7.5 or SAP BW/4HANA systems with the SAP BW Bridge via a tool-supported conversion. Customers can benefit from the access of S-API extractors and business content similarly to the SAP BW on-premises world, while moving SAP BW investments to SAP BW Bridge. At the same time, customers can adopt data products and insight apps with SAP Business Data Cloud.

 

Roadmap and Learning

In which data centers is SAP BDC available?

SAP Business Data Cloud will initially be available on various AWS data centers. By mid-year, we plan to offer several data centers for Google Cloud and in the second half of 2025 also data centers from Microsoft Azure.

 

What is the roadmap for SAP BDC?

SAP Business Data Cloud was launched with the SAP Business Unleashed virtual event on February 13, 2025, and is commercially available on a controlled availability basis. It is planned to be broadly available in April 2025 (GA). A more detailed roadmap is currently being developed and will be published on the SAP Road Map Explorer.

🔗 Explore our product plans on the Roadmap explorer

 

Are there any learning tutorials available?

To quickly learn more about SAP Business Data Cloud you can enjoy the SAP Business Data Cloud product tour, which is a click through demo to experience SAP Business Data Cloud's most powerful capabilities.

🔗 To get a level deeper you can learn more with tutorials on the learning journey.

entry.title: 🚀 Unlock the Power of Cloud, AI, and Business Transformation at SAP Sapphire 2025 in Madrid
entry.content:
From May 26 to 28, SAP Sapphire in Madrid will bring together the brightest minds, boldest innovations, and most impactful customer stories across the SAP ecosystem. Whether you're embarking on your RISE with SAP journey, transforming your support strategy with AI, or streamlining operations with SAP Cloud ALM, this is your chance to connect, learn, and get inspired.

If you're planning your agenda, make sure to explore the powerful lineup of sessions hosted by our SAP Customer Support & Cloud Lifecycle Management team. These sessions dive deep into real customer use cases, cutting-edge tools, and strategic insights that can help you unlock value, improve resilience, and stay ahead in today’s rapidly evolving business landscape.

📍Main Theater & Breakout Sessions – Tuesday, May 27

SER1211 | 01:00 PM | Room 10.17
Achieving a clean core with your RISE with SAP journey
Wieland Schreiner

Stefan Steinle, Bastian Kloiber (Bosch)

SER1215 | 03:00 PM | Room 10.18
Elevating your GROW with SAP journey with SAP Cloud ALM
Tonja Kehrer
Discover how SAP Cloud ALM can accelerate your GROW with SAP journey with real-time insights, demo-driven learning, and innovation strategies for scalable growth.

SER1214 | 03:30 PM | Room 10.18

Wilhelm Juette

🎯Why You Should Attend

But, If you are working on Insight Apps in the context of SAP Data Products, we recommend to note and get familiar with the content in the various blogs in order to communicate a uniform content and visual consistency with the finished apps to the outside world.

entry.title: New IDC Study: The Business Value of SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
The Business Value of SAP HANA Cloud, sponsored by SAP and conducted by IDC in 2025, explores the business value of SAP HANA Cloud for organizations modernizing their database environments. The research, based on customer interviews, calculates that companies using SAP HANA Cloud achieved significant benefits, including a high three-year ROI of 352% and an average annual benefit of $13.7 million. These gains stem from improved IT operational efficiency, enhanced agility in database deployment and development, optimized end-user productivity through better data insights, and ultimately, stronger business results. The study highlights specific areas of improvement, such as reduced costs, increased team productivity across various departments, and significant performance enhancements like faster queries and reduced downtime.

Read the full IDC study The Business Value of SAP HANA Cloud. 

Then, leverage the executive summary and snapshot to create a business case for your cloud database management system modernization initiative! 

Here is what you will learn:

What are the primary benefits organizations can expect from adopting SAP HANA Cloud, according to the IDC study?

The IDC study highlights significant business value achieved by organizations using SAP HANA Cloud. Key benefits include an average annual benefit of $820,000 per database, a 352% three-year return on investment, a 35% reduction in total cost of ownership over three years, and a 10.3-month payback period. Operationally, organizations experienced increased IT productivity, faster database deployments (38% quicker), reduced unplanned downtime (71% reduction), faster query speeds (31% faster), and improved efficiency across IT infrastructure management (33%), DBA (30%), and security teams (19%). Furthermore, analytics (25%) and development teams (30%) saw productivity gains, leading to the development of significantly more features and applications annually (154% more).

What were the key drivers behind the interviewed organizations' decisions to choose SAP HANA Cloud?

The organizations interviewed by IDC selected SAP HANA Cloud for several crucial reasons. A primary driver was the need for enhanced scalability to handle growing data volumes and support business growth. Integration capabilities with existing SAP and other systems were also a significant factor, simplifying data management and reducing complexity. Furthermore, the cloud-based nature of SAP HANA Cloud offered advantages in terms of cost-effectiveness, reduced operational overhead through automated maintenance, improved stability and support, and enhanced security and compliance features. The ability to achieve real-time insights and advance digital transformation were also important considerations.

How did SAP HANA Cloud impact the productivity of IT-related teams within the interviewed organizations?

SAP HANA Cloud had a substantial positive impact on the productivity of various IT teams. IT infrastructure management teams saw a 33% efficiency boost, requiring fewer full-time employees to manage their database environments. Helpdesk teams experienced a 35% reduction in annual tickets and a 57% reduction in average resolution time, leading to a significant decrease in the FTEs needed for support. DBA teams became 30% more efficient due to automation of tasks like backups and scaling, as well as improved tooling. Security teams also benefited from built-in security functionalities, achieving a 19% efficiency gain. These productivity improvements translated into significant annual cost savings for the organizations.

What tangible performance improvements in database operations did the study participants experience after implementing SAP HANA Cloud?

The study participants reported notable enhancements in database performance after adopting SAP HANA Cloud. They experienced 31% faster query speeds and a 29% improvement in response times. Database backups were also 27% quicker. Critically, unplanned database downtime was reduced by 71%, and the time to resolve outages decreased by 73%. These improvements not only enhanced operational reliability but also significantly reduced the impact of downtime on end-user productivity and potential revenue loss.

How did SAP HANA Cloud contribute to advancements in analytics and application development for the interviewed companies?

SAP HANA Cloud provided a robust platform for advancing analytics and application development. Analytics teams benefited from greater data accessibility, better searchability, real-time data access from a single source, and the ability to process multiple data types. This resulted in a 25% productivity boost and the ability to leverage significantly more data sources and larger data volumes. Application development teams experienced a 30% increase in productivity due to seamless integrations, reusable code and libraries, and sandboxed environments, leading to a 154% increase in the number of features and applications developed annually, including more complex AI/ML-powered applications.

What business enablement benefits did organizations realize by using SAP HANA Cloud?

The adoption of SAP HANA Cloud led to significant business enablement benefits. End users gained increased access to high-quality data and analytics, resulting in an 18% productivity gain. The platform facilitated better data-backed decision-making, more accurate and timely business forecasting, and improved customer insights. This contributed to an average of $39.27 million in additional annual gross revenue per organization. The ability to quickly generate business-critical insights and support sales efforts were also highlighted as key advantages.

What is the overall return on investment (ROI) and payback period that the interviewed organizations experienced with SAP HANA Cloud?

The IDC study calculated a compelling average three-year return on investment (ROI) of 352% for the organizations using SAP HANA Cloud. This significant ROI is attributed to a combination of cost savings from increased efficiencies, reduced operational costs, and business growth enabled by improved analytics and application development. The average payback period for the initial investment in SAP HANA Cloud was found to be a relatively short 10.3 months, demonstrating the rapid realization of its value.

Learn more:

  • SAP HANA Cloud
  • SAP HANA Cloud Modernization
  • IDC Business Value of SAP HANA Cloud Study
  • IDC Business Value of SAP HANA Cloud Snapshot
  • IDC Business Value of SAP HANA Cloud Executive Summary
